data.title: Grasshopper Bank Innovator Term Loan: Up to $200,000 With Soft Pull (No Collateral)
data.summary: Grasshopper Bank Innovator Term Loan: Up to $200,000 With Soft Pull (No Collateral)
data.description:
Use the Promo code 4d0b4cf3af05  to receive $200 bonus when you open a grasshopper - https://www.grasshopper.bank/banking-solutions/small-business-checking/


The video explains Grasshopper Bank’s Innovator Term Loan, which offers a one-time lump-sum business loan from $10,000 up to $200,000 with a soft credit pull, no hard credit check, and no collateral required. The application takes about 10 minutes, funds can be available within one business day, and the loan has a fixed 36-month term with no prepayment penalties and payments every 30 days. To apply, viewers must have a Grasshopper business checking account, and the script promotes using a referral link and code to open an account and potentially earn a $200 bonus with qualified activity. It notes that under $200,000 requests require no financial documents, but a personal guarantee is required. Eligibility includes having a Grasshopper account for at least six months and being in business for at least one year.

00:00 Fast Funding

data.title: Never Wait for a Paycheck Again: Instant Advances (No Credit Check)
data.summary: Never Wait for a Paycheck Again: Instant Advances (No Credit Check)
data.description:
Get Paid Faster on Gig Work, Stripe, Uber, YouTube & More (No Credit Check) | Sivo Advance - https://www.creditplug.com/blog-civo-advance-no-credit-check.html

Sivo, a Y Combinator–backed fintech company that advances money you’ve already earned so you don’t have to wait days or weeks for payouts. The host explains that gig workers, merchants, creators, real estate agents, and subscription-based businesses often have funds trapped in pending payouts, invoices, subscriptions, or commissions, and Sivo Advance turns those receivables into instant cash to help cover expenses or reinvest for growth. The script highlights that Sivo can advance earnings from integrated platforms such as Walmart, RE/MAX, Rappi, Stripe, and YouTube, using verified payout data to fund quickly. It emphasizes there is no credit check and approval is based on earnings already owed, not credit score or tax returns.

00:00 No Credit Check Hook

00:32 Meet Sivo Advance

data.title: CDFI Loans in 2026: Get Up to $250K+ With Low Credit, No Income (Top Local Funds Revealed)
data.summary: CDFI Loans in 2026: Get Up to $250K+ With Low Credit, No Income (Top Local Funds Revealed)
data.description:
CDFI Loans in 2026: Get Up to $250K+ With Low Credit, No Income (Top Local Funds Revealed)

The video explains how Community Development Financial Institutions (CDFIs) can be an underused alternative to major banks for people with low income and low credit, emphasizing that CDFIs focus on underserved borrowers and often require a manually submitted application package. It outlines what CDFIs want to see—business plan, projected financials, a clear use of funds, and a social-impact story—and notes some programs may ask for a major-bank denial letter. The host shares examples in the DC/MD/VA area, including Washington Area Community Investment Fund ($45,000 loan + $5,000 grant with no credit check), Locus Impact microloans, Latino Economic Development Center (up to $250,000 with some 0% options and credit-builder products), FSC First (multiple PG County programs including $25,000–$350,000 and VLT Flex Fund $25,000–$250,000), Millennium Management Group (550 minimum score, up to $2M), and

data.title: How a New LLC Got a $20,000 Line of Credit in Minutes
data.summary: How a New LLC Got a $20,000 Line of Credit in Minutes
data.description:
Get CDFI Funding Sequence - https://www.creditplug.com/cdfi-advisory.html
How a New LLC Got a $20,000 BlueVine Line of Credit in Minutes (Cashflow-Based Funding) - https://www.bluevine.com/partner/credit-plug

The video shows a client approval for a $20,000 BlueVine cashflow-based line of credit, explaining that qualification is driven mainly by business checking activity (with a soft pull for identity) and can work with a relatively new LLC after a few months of transactions. It reviews the terms (26-week draw term and a 0.97% weekly rate based on weekly balance), demonstrates how draws are entered, and notes additional BlueVine services. The speaker explains the client was set up with QuickBooks first so BlueVine could pull and underwrite business data through QuickBooks, resulting in approval in about five minutes.
